source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Semiconductors such as silicon are used to fabricate solar cells, devices that generate electric energy when exposed to sunlight. Solar cells are often coated with a transparent thin film to minimize reflective losses. A silicon solar cell is coated with a thin film of silicon monoxide for this purpose. Assuming normal incidence, determine the minimum thickness of the film that will produce the least reflection at a wavelength of \( 552 \, \mathrm{nm} \).

issues: metadata.image_paths differs from top-level image_paths: metadata uses inline://phyx_image_1457 while top-level uses a filesystem path.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is registered as image-reliant
question_text does not provide the refractive index needed to derive 95.2 nm from 552 nm, so some required information likely comes from the image or omitted context
an image asset exists and registry_passed=true, so the image is a valid part of the sample package
multi_step: solving requires applying thin-film interference/anti-reflection conditions, not simple lookup
the solver must combine wavelength information with film-thickness logic and the film's optical property to reach the minimum-thickness result
multiple-choice format with plausible distractors suggests a structured calculation rather than direct extraction
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text is a specific numeric value ('95.2 nm')
choices are provided, and the answer matches one option exactly (B)
the result can be checked by a physics rule/calculation pipeline, and registry_passed=true
